<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     HOW THIS FILE WORKS (for reference — not instructions to the user)
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     The LLM runner generates this file as STEP 1 of every MWS run:
       1. Searches the web across 8 news categories (trailing 7 days)
       2. Rates each item HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW per policy materiality rules
       3. Assesses signal–news interaction per affected sleeve
       4. Writes override candidates for HIGH contradictions
       5. Snapshots VIX + drawdown regime from mws_tracker.json
       6. Then proceeds to systematic recommendation

     Governance rules (from mws_policy.json → news_intelligence):
       • News CANNOT override hard drawdown rules, caps/floors, Bucket A, turnover caps
       • News CAN trigger discretionary_override_review for any trade
       • Max tilt from any single news item: bounded by news_weight_governance
       • If web search unavailable: log "NO DATA" and proceed on systematic signal only

     Search queries and rating rules: mws_policy.json → news_intelligence.generation_protocol
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
